title: Stage 06d — Verification Beyond Tests
description: G7 — property-based testing, mutation testing, and/or formal verification applied to the changed code after stage-06 (qa) PASS. Records WHICH methods were attempted, WHICH were skipped (with reason), and WHAT was found. PASS requires blocking_findings to be empty. Read-only on production code; writes verification-report.md, the gate, and any new property tests under src/tests/property/.

methods_attempted.description: Verification methods the verifier actually ran. Subset of ["property", "mutation", "formal"]. Each entry implies a corresponding object (property_based / mutation / formal) populated below. A method that the verifier intended to run but couldn't (e.g. tool not installed) is `attempted_but_blocked` — record it here AND surface a warning.

methods_skipped.description: Verification methods the verifier deliberately did not run, with reasons. Each entry: { method, reason }. A method with no candidates ("no pure functions in diff") is a legitimate skip; "didn't have time" is not.

candidates_inventoried.description: Number of functions/modules the verifier classified in Phase 1's inventory table. A run with zero candidates means the diff was all glue/UI — record reasons in methods_skipped[].

property_based.description: Populated when 'property' is in methods_attempted. null otherwise.

cases_tried.description: Total cases the framework explored (often 100–10000 per property).

mutation.description: Populated when 'mutation' is in methods_attempted. null otherwise.

score.description: killed / (generated - timed_out). 80% is a defensible new-code threshold; 100% is suspicious.

threshold.description: Pre-declared kill-ratio threshold the verifier committed to BEFORE running. Audit-grade: prevents goal-post moving.

target.description: Source path(s) the mutator ran against.

formal.description: Populated when 'formal' is in methods_attempted. null otherwise.

findings_count.description: Total findings across all methods (blocking + non_blocking + methodology).

blocking_findings.description: Findings that must be addressed before sign-off. A surviving mutant on a critical path, a property counterexample to a stated invariant, a formal counterexample to a safety property. Non-empty → stage FAIL → implementer addresses → re-run. Analogous to red-team's must_address_before_peer_review[].

counterexample.description: Verbatim shrunk counterexample or mutation operator.

non_blocking_findings.description: Known gaps, ticketed follow-ups, edge cases at the limit of the spec. Recorded but don't fail the stage.
